verses.text: The LORD will yet have mercy on Jacob, set them in their own land, and give them rest from their sorrow and bondage. Judgment on Babylon means deliverance for God's people. He overthrows the oppressor to bring His own to rest.

The LORD will yet have mercy on Jacob, set them in their own land, and give them rest from their sorrow and bondage. Judgment on Babylon means deliverance for God's people. He overthrows the oppressor to bring His own to rest.

The LORD will yet have mercy on Jacob, set them in their own land, and give them rest from their sorrow and bondage. Judgment on Babylon means deliverance for God's people. He overthrows the oppressor to bring His own to rest.

Israel takes up a taunt: 'How hath the oppressor ceased!' The whole earth is at rest and breaks into singing now the tyrant is gone. The fall of the cruel brings the world relief. Even the trees rejoice that the destroyer is silenced.

Israel takes up a taunt: 'How hath the oppressor ceased!' The whole earth is at rest and breaks into singing now the tyrant is gone. The fall of the cruel brings the world relief. Even the trees rejoice that the destroyer is silenced.
The grave stirs to meet the fallen king; the dead kings rise to greet him; 'thy pomp is brought down to the grave.' Death levels the mighty. The tyrant who terrified the living joins the worms below.
The grave stirs to meet the fallen king; the dead kings rise to greet him; 'thy pomp is brought down to the grave.' Death levels the mighty. The tyrant who terrified the living joins the worms below.
The grave stirs to meet the fallen king; the dead kings rise to greet him; 'thy pomp is brought down to the grave.' Death levels the mighty. The tyrant who terrified the living joins the worms below.

'How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer,' who said, 'I will ascend into heaven... I will be like the most High.' Pride aspired to God's throne. Here is the very spirit of rebellion, which seeks to dethrone God and is itself cast down.

'How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer,' who said, 'I will ascend into heaven... I will be like the most High.' Pride aspired to God's throne. Here is the very spirit of rebellion, which seeks to dethrone God and is itself cast down.

'How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer,' who said, 'I will ascend into heaven... I will be like the most High.' Pride aspired to God's throne. Here is the very spirit of rebellion, which seeks to dethrone God and is itself cast down.
Yet he is brought down to hell, gazed upon as the man who shook the earth and made the world a wilderness. His boast ends in scorn. The proud who sought the heights are stared at in the pit.
Yet he is brought down to hell, gazed upon as the man who shook the earth and made the world a wilderness. His boast ends in scorn. The proud who sought the heights are stared at in the pit.
Yet he is brought down to hell, gazed upon as the man who shook the earth and made the world a wilderness. His boast ends in scorn. The proud who sought the heights are stared at in the pit.
Other kings lie honoured in their tombs, but he is cast out unburied, 'as an abominable branch,' for he destroyed his land. Even a grave is denied him. Tyranny earns a shameful, forgotten end.
Other kings lie honoured in their tombs, but he is cast out unburied, 'as an abominable branch,' for he destroyed his land. Even a grave is denied him. Tyranny earns a shameful, forgotten end.

God decrees slaughter for his sons and the cutting off of Babylon's name and remnant, sweeping her with 'the besom of destruction.' His house and city are erased. The judgment on pride reaches to its very root.

The LORD has sworn to break the Assyrian in His own land and lift his yoke; 'who shall disannul it?' His purpose stands sure. What God has determined, no power on earth can overturn.

In the year Ahaz died, Philistia is warned not to rejoice, for a worse rod will come; but the LORD 'hath founded Zion,' and His poor shall trust in it. Enemies' relief is short-lived. God's people find refuge in the city He has established.
